arts.ttl: Ill-stricken Carranza avoids extra time at Milan by scoring goal to send Feyenoord to last 16
arts.sum: Recovering from illness, Feyenoord forward Julián Carranza knew that even after coming on as a late substitute he would struggle to play extra time against AC Milan. Carranza was brought off the bench in the 64th minute, with Milan leading 1-0 — following a goal from former Feyenoord forward Santiago Giménez after 40 seconds — but also at a numerical disadvantage after the dismissal of Rossoneri defender Theo Hernandez.

arts.ttl: Santiago Gimenez Sets New Champions League Record Despite Round of 16 Exit
arts.sum: Santiago Giménez passed Chicharito Hernández and Hugo Sánchez for the most goals by a Mexican player in a single Champions League season.

arts.ttl: Champions League playoffs: Feyenoord’s Carranza sends 10-man Milan crashing out
arts.sum: Italian side pegged back to 1-1 and lose 2-1 on aggregate Hernandez sent off after dive earned him second yellow Milan were left to rue Théo Hernandez’s sending-off following a dive as Feyenoord pegged them back on the night and advanced 2-1 on aggregate to the Champions League last 16 with a 1-1 draw in the second leg of their playoff. Hernandez earned his second yellow card for the seven-time European champions for a dive in the 51st minute when Milan was dominating. The Italian side had led from almost the start, after forward Santiago Giménez’s first-minute goal had levelled the tie against his former team. Feyenoord had won the first leg of the playoff 1-0 at home last week. However, Milan’s defence looked anything but assured, particularly after Hernandez was dismissed. Hugo Bueno crossed for Julián Carranza to head Feyenoord level on the night in the 73rd minute, allowing the Dutch team to retake the aggregate lead. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Christian Pulisic and AC Milan “cheated” out of the Champions League
arts.sum: Christian Pulisic’s dreams of winning the Champions League for a second time in his career are over after AC Milan were disappointing eliminated in the knockout phase playoff by Dutch club Feyenoord at San Siro. Referee Szymon Marciniak immediately spotted the full-back’s blatant dive and showed him a second yellow card, changing the entire complexion of the game.
